name: Superhuman
purpose: AI-powered email client layered on Gmail / Microsoft 365 — AI-assisted drafting, triage, and inbox management (Ask AI, Instant Reply, Auto Summarize, Write with AI).
dataTypes: Faculty/staff email content, calendar, and contacts from an individual Gmail or Microsoft 365 mailbox, connected per user. No student information system (SIS) access.

notes.co_audit: SOC 2 Type II compliant; also holds ISO/IEC 27001, 27017 (cloud security), 27018 (PII in the cloud), and 27701 (privacy) certifications. Full SOC 2 report is available via the Trust Center on request/NDA, not published in full. (source: https://trust.superhuman.com/)
notes.co_trust: Trust Center at trust.superhuman.com (SafeBase-powered) hosts a downloadable SOC 2 report, ISO certificates, a pentest report, the sub-processor list, and BC/DR documentation — substantive, not just marketing. (source: https://trust.superhuman.com/)
notes.co_pentest:
States infrastructure undergoes "annual third-party penetration testing," and a Pentest Report is listed as a downloadable Trust Center artifact — but the report is gated and its exact test date wasn't independently confirmable from public sources. (source: https://trust.superhuman.com/)
Vendor's compliance team confirmed by email: "The latest Penetration test was in November 2025" — within the 12-month window, resolving the earlier "date unconfirmed" gap. (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
notes.d_transit: "All data is encrypted at rest and in transit, with particularly sensitive data encrypted additionally at the application level." TLS for network traffic; internal traffic uses Google Cloud ALTS. (source: https://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-soc-2-compliant-data-privacy/)
notes.d_access: States "we use the principle of least privilege: services and users are granted the minimal set of permissions required to do their job." MFA is listed as a Trust Center product-security feature, but no explicit public statement confirms mandatory MFA for Superhuman's own employees specifically — ask the vendor to confirm directly. (source: https://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-soc-2-compliant-data-privacy/)
notes.d_residency: Public sources conflict: the company blog states infrastructure "runs fully on infrastructure managed by Google [Cloud]", while the Trust Center's own sub-processor list separately lists AWS for "data hosting and product infrastructure." Both are reputable US providers, but the discrepancy and the lack of a disclosed region should be clarified directly with the vendor before approval. (source: https://superhuman.com/legal/subprocessors)
notes.ai_training: Conflicting public statements found. Superhuman's blog states "Neither Mail nor any vendor trains on your data" and that they have "not and will not opt-in to sharing user data with OpenAI for model training." However, Grammarly's privacy policy (which now governs Superhuman post-acquisition) states users "can decide whether Superhuman can use your user content to train our AI models by adjusting the available training control(s)" — implying training is possible by default unless a user opts out. This contradiction is a critical open item: get it resolved in writing, in the DPA or a signed addendum, before approval. (source: https://grammarly.com/privacy-policy)
notes.ai_subproc: Sub-processor list at superhuman.com/legal/subprocessors discloses OpenAI, Anthropic, and Azure (Microsoft) as "Generative AI provider," all US-located. (source: https://superhuman.com/legal/subprocessors)
notes.ai_optout: Confirmed Zero Data Retention agreement with OpenAI specifically ("data will not be saved or retained by OpenAI"). No public confirmation that equivalent zero-retention terms apply to the other two disclosed AI sub-processors (Anthropic, Azure) — ask the vendor to confirm in writing for all three. (source: https://blog.superhuman.com/superhuman-soc-2-compliant-data-privacy/)
notes.p_retention: DPA commits to returning or securely destroying customer personal data at termination upon written request (subject to legal retention requirements). AI-feature-specific retention is also documented: Ask AI queries "stored to help improve quality and allow for debugging"; Instant Reply / Auto Summarize queries retained 90 days; Write with AI queries not stored. (source: https://superhuman.com/dpa)
notes.p_dpa: Standard DPA publicly posted at superhuman.com/dpa, covering GDPR, UK GDPR, and Swiss DPA, structured for Enterprise/Education tiers. (source: https://superhuman.com/dpa)
notes.p_flow:
No public data-flow diagram located on the Trust Center, superhuman.com, or help docs as of this review — request one directly from the vendor. (source: https://trust.superhuman.com/)
Vendor's compliance team confirmed by email that a data flow diagram exists: "included in the Security Whitepaper / architecture & data flow diagrams that are available via the Superhuman Mail Trust Center." Request and retain the actual document via the Trust Center for the compliance file. (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
notes.a_scope:
Sign-in is per individual Google/Microsoft account (not an org-wide admin consent grant), and Google Workspace admins can block the app entirely at the domain level — but no public documentation explicitly confirms whether Superhuman ever requests Google domain-wide delegation or Microsoft tenant-wide app permissions rather than single-mailbox access. Left unanswered deliberately: confirm the exact connection scope directly with the vendor and district IT before approving anything broader than single-mailbox use. (source: https://help.superhuman.com/)
Not the original question the vendor answered directly, but their OAuth-scope confirmation directly resolves this critical gap too: "We do not request tenant-wide admin privileges... access is further constrained by your own Conditional Access and IdP policies" describes delegated, per-user sign-in — not domain-wide/tenant-wide delegation. Inferred from the OAuth answer rather than a literal answer to this exact question; worth a one-line written confirmation from the vendor addressed specifically to connection scope before finalizing. (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
notes.a_oauth:
Exact OAuth scope strings aren't published. Confirmed only that sign-in requests access to Gmail/Outlook mail, calendar, and contacts, with help documentation referring to a "grant full permissions" prompt — broader than a narrow read-only scope, though functionally explainable for an email client. (source: https://help.superhuman.com/)
Vendor's compliance team confirmed by email: "Superhuman Mail is designed to use delegated, least-privilege OAuth/Microsoft Graph permissions, limited to the minimum scopes required to operate: Exchange Online mail, calendar, contacts, and basic identity/profile for the signed-in user. We do not request tenant-wide admin privileges or access to SharePoint, OneDrive, or Teams." Upgraded from "broad but explainable" now that exact scopes are itemized. (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
notes.a_sis: Superhuman is a personal email client (Gmail/Microsoft 365) with no integration point to student information systems (Banner, Canvas, or similar) in its public feature set.
notes.o_breach:
Trust Center references "designated response personnel, forensic retainers, and formal incident reporting processes" as part of its BC/DR documentation, but no standalone public incident-response/breach-notification policy page was found beyond that summary. (source: https://trust.superhuman.com/)
Vendor's compliance team confirmed by email a formally documented Incident Response Plan, Security Incident Management Procedure, and Personal Data Breach Notification process, with 24/7 on-call, severity-based triage, and GDPR-aligned notification "within 72 hours of determining a reportable personal-data breach, with shorter timelines — e.g., 24 hours — available by contract." (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
notes.o_bcp:
A public status page exists (status.superhuman.com) showing real-time component status, but no published numeric SLA or uptime commitment (e.g. 99.9%) was found in public sources. (source: https://status.superhuman.com/)
Vendor's compliance team confirmed by email a documented BC/DR plan tested at least annually, with "published targets of RTO 48 hours and RPO 24 hours," reviewed as part of SOC 2. Not a numeric uptime SLA, but a formal, tested continuity commitment. (source: Superhuman compliance team, email reply to follow-up questions, Jul 2026)
